topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara stared at the canvas, the vibrant blues and sharp reds of her latest piece mocking her from the stretched linen. She ran a hand through her already messy hair, the gesture tight and jerky. Then she saw it—a tiny, almost invisible line of black ink tracing the outline of her central figure’s eye. A slow, cold tightening began in her chest, spreading outward until her jaw felt locked. She leaned in close, squinting, then pulled back sharply, her breath catching in a shallow gasp. "What in the world?" she muttered, the sound brittle. She circled the painting twice, her movements becoming stiff and precise, as if trying to physically erase the sight. The air in the studio suddenly felt too thick, too heavy.

[story 2]

Marcus picked up the print, his thumb tracing the texture of the paper, a familiar wave of irritation washing over him. He was reviewing his portfolio, a routine task, when his gaze snagged on a detail in the background of his most recent landscape. A deliberate, intricate pattern, clearly a tattoo, was woven into the shadow work. A low growl escaped his throat, a sound he immediately tried to smother with a cough. He dropped the print onto the desk with unnecessary force, the resulting clatter echoing in the quiet room. He stood up abruptly, pacing the small space, his shoulders hunched, his eyes darting around as if searching for the source of this unwelcome intrusion.

[story 3]

Chloe sighed, a long, drawn-out expulsion of air that carried the weight of profound displeasure. She was examining the final touches on her portrait, meticulously adjusting the highlights, when her peripheral vision caught the detail. Beneath the delicate rendering of the subject’s collarbone, a stark, inked design was clearly visible. A muscle ticked violently in her cheek. She stopped breathing for a beat, her fingers freezing mid-air above the brush. A distinct heat rose in her neck, and she straightened up, her posture instantly rigid. "Seriously?" she whispered to the empty room, her voice dangerously level, laced with a sharp edge that made the silence feel charged and unwelcome.
